text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST AND PET-CT (18F-FDG)

CLINICAL INDICATION: 52-year-old female with FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (5382insC). Status post primary debulking surgery with R0 resection followed by 6 cycles of carboplatin/paclitaxel chemotherapy. End-of-treatment restaging. CA-125 18 U/mL.

COMPARISON: Prior CT abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 10 weeks ago (mid-cycle restaging after cycle 3), and staging CT performed at time of initial diagnosis.

TECHNIQUE: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed with 100 mL of iohexol IV contrast (portal venous phase) on a 64-slice multidetector CT scanner. Contiguous 5 mm axial images obtained from lung apices through pubic symphysis with multiplanar reformats. PET-CT performed on a dedicated PET/CT scanner following IV administration of 10.2 mCi of 18F-FDG, with patient fasting for 6 hours prior to injection (blood glucose at time of injection: 94 mg/dL). Whole-body images acquired from skull vertex to mid-thigh at 45 minutes post-injection. CT component of PET/CT used for attenuation correction and anatomic localization.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:
Lungs: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pulmonary nodules. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. The airways are patent to the segmental level. No hilar or mediastinal adenopathy. The visualized cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. Pericardium is unremarkable.

Mediastinum/Hila: No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. Mediastinal structures are midline. Thyroid gland is not well evaluated on this study; no discrete nodularity noted in the thoracic inlet on scout imaging.

Breast tissue: Dense fibroglandular tissue bilaterally; no dominant mass or suspicious lesion identified. Dedicated breast imaging recommended separately per clinical protocol.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. No hepatic surface nodularity or perihepatitic implants. No focal hepatic parenchymal lesions. The previously noted liver surface implant (0.6 cm, excised at time of cytoreductive surgery) shows no residual or recurrent focus at the operative bed on the right hepatic lobe surface. Portal vein and hepatic veins are patent. Intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated.

Biliary: Gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No cholelithiasis. Common bile duct is not dilated.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Visualized pancreatic parenchyma is unremarkable. No ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding.

Adrenal glands: Both adrenal glands are thin and unremarkable. No adrenal masses.

Kidneys/Ureters: Both kidneys demonstrate normal size and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal lesions. Normal excretion of contrast bilaterally. No periureteral masses.

Bowel: The stomach is decompressed and unremarkable. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber and wall thickness without obstruction or mural thickening. No ileitis pattern. The colon is decompressed without wall thickening. Appendix was surgically removed (appendectomy noted at time of cytoreduction); no residual appendiceal tissue or stump abscess.

Retroperitoneum/Mesentery: No soft tissue masses. No ascites. The mesentery appears clean with no implants or nodularity along the mesenteric leaves or root. The retroperitoneum is clear without visible nodal masses. The previously enlarged para-aortic lymph nodes (up to 1.1 cm at presentation, with 2/14 nodes positive at surgery) are not visualized as discrete pathologic nodes on the current study. No confluent nodal soft tissue.

Omentum: No residual omental tissue identified; the omental space in the infracolic compartment shows post-omentectomy changes without nodular recurrence or fat-stranding implants. The area of the prior omental cake is now occupied by normal-appearing bowel and fat, without masses.

Diaphragm: Diaphragmatic contours are smooth bilaterally. No nodular thickening along the diaphragmatic surface. The diaphragmatic peritoneum that was stripped surgically shows post-procedural irregularity on both sides, but no hypermetabolic or mass lesion is identified. The previously documented diaphragmatic implants are not recapitulated.

PELVIS:
Uterus and adnexa: The patient has undergone total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy; surgical bed is visualized without recurrent masses. No residual uterine tissue. No adnexal masses or cystic structures. Vaginal vault appears intact without hyperenhancement or mass.

Pelvic peritoneum/cul-de-sac: No free fluid in the cul-de-sac or paravesical spaces. No peritoneal thickening or nodular implants along the pelvic sidewalls, bladder serosa, or rectosigmoid mesentery. The previously involved pelvic peritoneal surfaces (right and left pelvic sidewall, right external iliac nodal region) show expected post-surgical fatty change without new nodularity.

Pelvic lymph nodes: No pathologically enlarged pelvic lymph nodes. The previously involved right external iliac (1.4 cm) and left internal iliac (1.2 cm) nodal groups are not identified as distinct enlarged nodes. No new lymphadenopathy.

Bladder: Decompressed. Wall thickness unremarkable.

Rectum/sigmoid: No wall thickening or perirectal adenopathy.

Bony structures/musculature: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No fractures. Postsurgical soft tissue changes in the anterior abdominal wall are stable. No abdominal wall hernia.

---

PET-CT FINDINGS:

Background physiologic FDG distribution is normal. Expected tracer activity in brain, heart, liver, kidneys, ureters, and bladder.

Thorax: No areas of abnormal FDG uptake in the lungs, mediastinum, pleura, or chest wall. No hypermetabolic pulmonary nodules.

Abdomen: No abnormal metabolic activity in the liver, spleen, or retroperitoneum. The previously documented liver surface implant site on the right hepatic lobe shows background-level activity (SUVmax 1.4). No hypermetabolic foci along the mesenteric leaves, omental bed, or paracolic gutters. No abnormal activity in the para-aortic nodal region.

Diaphragm/upper abdomen: The diaphragmatic surfaces show no FDG-avid nodularity. Post-surgical changes are metabolically quiescent.

Pelvis: No FDG-avid foci within the pelvis. The surgical bed in the hysterectomy/BSO region demonstrates background-level metabolic activity (SUVmax 1.4). No hypermetabolic pelvic sidewall or cul-de-sac lesions. No abnormal activity in the previously involved pelvic or iliac nodal stations.

Other: No focal areas of abnormal FDG uptake in the osseous structures, soft tissues, head, neck, or extremities. No unexpected hypermetabolic foci identified anywhere on whole-body survey.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No FDG-avid disease identified anywhere on whole-body PET survey. Complete metabolic response.

2. No CT-evident peritoneal implants, ascites, lymphadenopathy, or parenchymal organ metastases. The previously extensive peritoneal carcinomatosis, omental cake, bilateral ovarian masses, diaphragmatic implants, and pelvic/para-aortic lymphadenopathy documented at staging are absent. This is consistent with the known R0 surgical cytoreduction followed by completed systemic chemotherapy.

3. Post-surgical changes in the abdomen and pelvis are as expected and unchanged from the prior mid-treatment scan.

4. No evidence of recurrent or residual ovarian malignancy by either CT or PET-CT criteria.

Interpreting Radiologist: [SIGNED]
Attending Radiologist: [SIGNED]
Report finalized.
